prompt: I need to understand how our production Kubernetes cluster handles pod scheduling under load, but I absolutely cannot disrupt the live workloads. Design a read-only observation plan that lets me see scheduling decisions without affecting them.

We want to test our disaster recovery plan but can't afford to actually failover production. Design a shadow exercise where we observe what would happen during a region failure without actually triggering one.

I need to audit how our production API handles authentication edge cases — expired tokens, malformed headers, rate limit approaches — without generating real traffic or affecting real users. Design the ghost observation.

Before migrating our database, I want to see how the new schema would perform with production query patterns. Design a read-replica based approach where I can test queries against real data without any write risk.

Our team wants to evaluate a new logging framework but needs to see it handling real production log volume first. Design a shadow pipeline that mirrors production logs to the new framework without touching the existing logging infrastructure.

I need to understand how users actually navigate our application — click paths, feature usage, session duration — but our analytics are broken. Set up a read-only observation layer that captures this without modifying the application code.

We're considering changing our CDN provider. Before switching, I want to shadow-route 1% of production traffic through the new CDN and compare performance metrics without affecting user experience. Design this dry run.

Our security team wants to understand the blast radius of a compromised service account. I need to map what that account can access in production without actually exercising those permissions. Design the reconnaissance plan.

Before deploying a new caching layer, I want to observe the current cache hit/miss patterns in production to validate my assumptions about what to cache. Design a passive observation approach using existing metrics and logs.

We need to validate that our new firewall rules won't break anything before applying them. Set up a dry-run mode where we can see which traffic would be blocked or allowed without actually enforcing the rules.
